title: Dublin 3-Day City Break
metaTitle: Dublin 3-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Dublin, Ireland: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Guinness Storehouse and Book of Kells & Trinity Old Library. Prices from $974 per person (estimate).
highlights: Book of Kells & Trinity Old Library
Howth fishing village and cliff walk
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Dublin, Ireland — Temperate maritime climate — mild year-round with cool winters (highs around 8°C) and mild summers (highs around 19-20°C); rain is possible on any given day regardless of season. May to September for the mildest, driest weather and the longest daylight hours. This itinerary blends Guinness Storehouse, Book of Kells & Trinity Old Library, Temple Bar with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Guinness Storehouse, Book of Kells & Trinity Old Library, Temple Bar
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Dublin
itinerary.description: Land at Dublin Airport (DUB) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Guinness Storehouse — Interactive brewery tour ending in the rooftop Gravity Bar. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Dublin: Book of Kells & Trinity Old Library & Dublin Castle
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Book of Kells & Trinity Old Library — Illuminated medieval manuscript in the Long Room library. In the afternoon, continue to Dublin Castle — Former seat of British rule with State Apartments. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Book of Kells & Trinity Old Library

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Kilmainham Gaol or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to DUB for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book Guinness Storehouse and Book of Kells tickets online for timed entry.
Pubs get extremely busy on weekend nights, so arrive early for a seat.
The Leap Visitor Card offers unlimited travel and is worth it for multi-day stays.
Major museums like the National Gallery and National Museum are completely free.
Day trips to Wicklow or Howth are easy by DART or coach without renting a car.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
A waterproof jacket regardless of season
Layers for weather that changes within a single day
A warm sweater for cool evenings even in summer
thingsToDo: Book of Kells & Trinity Old Library
Cycling the Great Dublin Bike Ride routes
thingsToAvoid: Overpriced pubs directly on Temple Bar's main strip — walk a block further for better value.
Assuming taxis beat public transport during rush hour traffic.
Leaving Guinness Storehouse tickets to the last minute in peak season.
Underestimating how quickly sunny mornings turn to rain.
Walking against the local flow on footpaths and escalators.
topRestaurants: The Church Café Bar Restaurant — Modern Irish ($$)
Fade Street Social — Modern Irish Tapas ($$$)
The Winding Stair — Modern Irish Seafood ($$$)
The Brazen Head — Traditional Irish Pub Fare ($$)
hiddenGems: Marsh's Library, Ireland's oldest public library
adventureActivities: Cycling the Great Dublin Bike Ride routes
luxuryExperiences: Private whiskey and Guinness tasting tour
Stay at The Shelbourne overlooking St Stephen's Green
Private guided Trinity College and Book of Kells tour before opening
Chauffeured day trip to the Wicklow Mountains
honeymoonExperiences: Dinner cruise on the River Liffey
Couples day trip to Powerscourt Estate and Gardens
Evening at a traditional Irish music pub session
budgetExperiences: Free entry to the National Museum and National Gallery
Wandering Temple Bar and Grafton Street buskers
Free walking tour of the city centre
Phoenix Park picnic and wild deer spotting

festivalCalendar.name: Bloomsday
festivalCalendar.timing: June 16, city-wide celebrations of James Joyce's Ulysses

festivalCalendar.name: Temple Bar TradFest
festivalCalendar.timing: Late January, traditional Irish music festival

localEtiquette: Buying "rounds" of drinks at the pub is customary — reciprocate if you're included in one.
Friendly small talk with strangers, taxi drivers and shopkeepers is normal and welcomed.
Queueing is expected and taken seriously.
"Slagging" — light, self-deprecating banter — is a sign of friendliness, not rudeness.
Tipping around 10-15% in sit-down restaurants is appreciated but not obligatory.
localTransportGuide: Dublin's Luas tram lines, DART coastal rail and Dublin Bus network are covered by a tap-to-pay Leap Card, far cheaper than single tickets. The compact city centre is very walkable, while the DART is the easiest way to reach coastal towns like Howth and Dun Laoghaire.
bestTimeToVisit: May to September for the mildest, driest weather and the longest daylight hours.
weather: Temperate maritime climate — mild year-round with cool winters (highs around 8°C) and mild summers (highs around 19-20°C); rain is possible on any given day regardless of season.
visaInformation: Ireland is outside Schengen but shares a Common Travel Area with the UK. US, Canadian, Australian, New Zealand and most EU passport holders can visit visa-free for up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need an Irish visa arranged in advance — a UK or Schengen visa does not cover Ireland.
averageFlightTime: ~10.5h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Dublin?
faqs.answer: Ireland is outside Schengen but shares a Common Travel Area with the UK. US, Canadian, Australian, New Zealand and most EU passport holders can visit visa-free for up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need an Irish visa arranged in advance — a UK or Schengen visa does not cover Ireland.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Dublin?
faqs.answer: May to September for the mildest, driest weather and the longest daylight hours. Temperate maritime climate — mild year-round with cool winters (highs around 8°C) and mild summers (highs around 19-20°C); rain is possible on any given day regardless of season.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Dublin safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Dublin is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Dublin?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.
